name: Alpine.js
description: A rugged, minimal JavaScript framework for composing behavior directly in your markup

description.value: A rugged, minimal JavaScript framework for composing behavior directly in your markup

initial-release.comment: Created by Caleb Porzio in November 2019

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained with 291 contributors; regular releases in 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Declarative reactivity via x-data; uses raw DOM operations without virtual DOM

component-model.comment: Behavior is composed directly in HTML markup using x- directives

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative directives in HTML for reactive behavior

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in x-data for local state and Alpine.store() for global state

opinionatedness.comment: Minimal and unopinionated; just 18 directives, 6 properties, and 2 methods

backward-compatibility.comment: Breaking changes minimized between v3.x releases

compiles-to.comment: Pure JavaScript runtime; no compilation required

typescript-support.comment: Community TypeScript definitions available; usable with TypeScript but not written in it

template-syntax.comment: Directives embedded directly in HTML attributes (x-data, x-show, x-bind, etc.)

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; uses standard CSS

build-required.comment: No build step required; can be added via single CDN script tag

reactive-primitives.comment: x-data creates reactive state objects; Alpine.reactive() for manual reactivity

computed-values.comment: JavaScript getters within x-data enable reactive computed values

two-way-binding.comment: x-model directive provides two-way binding for form inputs

event-handling.comment: x-on (or @) directive for declarative event binding with modifiers

conditional-rendering.comment: x-show for CSS display toggling; x-if for DOM removal/insertion

list-rendering.comment: x-for directive for iterating over arrays in templates

component-slots.comment: No built-in slot/content projection system; Alpine enhances existing HTML

teleport-portal.comment: x-teleport directive to render content elsewhere in the DOM

suspense.comment: No built-in async loading boundaries; handle async manually

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in context/DI; use Alpine.store() for shared state

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: x-ref directive provides direct access to DOM elements via $refs

official-router.comment: No official router; Alpine is designed for enhancing server-rendered pages, not SPAs

router-name.comment: No official router; community solutions include alpinejs-app and single-spa-alpinejs

built-in-state.comment: x-data directive creates reactive local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: Alpine.store() provides global reactive state accessible across components

global-state-library.comment: Built-in global state via Alpine.store(); optional $persist plugin for persistence

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Alpine.js Devtools browser extension for Chrome and Firefox

state-persistence.comment: Official $persist plugin saves state to localStorage/sessionStorage

official-cli.comment: No CLI needed; add via CDN or npm install

hot-module-replacement.comment: Not applicable; typically used without build step

browser-devtools.comment: Alpine.js Devtools for Chrome and Firefox; inspect and edit component data

vs-code-extension.comment: Multiple VS Code extensions: Alpine.js IntelliSense, Alpinejs Support, syntax highlighting

error-messages.comment: Clear runtime errors with component context

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with clear examples; comprehensive directive reference and plugin docs

interactive-tutorial.comment: freeCodeCamp interactive tutorial; no official first-party tutorial

playground.comment: No official playground; CodePen and JSFiddle commonly used

bundle-size-core.comment: ~16.3 kB minified + gzipped (v3.x); grew from ~9 kB in v2

bundle-size-full.comment: ~20 kB with common plugins (Persist, Collapse, Focus)

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; uses direct DOM operations for minimal overhead

tree-shaking.comment: Plugins are separate imports enabling tree-shaking

code-splitting.comment: No built-in code splitting; typically loaded as single script

lazy-components.comment: No component lazy loading; all behavior initialized on page load

ssr-capable.comment: Designed for client-side enhancement of server-rendered HTML; no SSR support

partial-hydration.comment: No hydration concept; enhances existing HTML directly

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not designed for benchmark-style DOM operations; optimized for small component interactivity

component-libraries.comment: Fewer dedicated libraries; commonly paired with Tailwind UI, Headless UI patterns

meta-framework.comment: No meta-framework; commonly used with Laravel Livewire for full-stack development

major-companies-using.comment: Widely adopted in Laravel/PHP community; created by Livewire author Caleb Porzio

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven; created and maintained by Caleb Porzio with GitHub sponsors

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Questions exist under alpine.js tag; exact count not verified

job-market-demand.comment: Growing niche, especially in Laravel ecosystem; not standalone job requirement

testing-library-name.comment: Community alpine-test-utils library; Alpine repo uses Cypress + Vitest

component-testing.comment: Via alpine-test-utils with Jest/Vitest and JSDOM

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Cypress and Playwright; Alpine repo uses Cypress for E2E

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; works in WebViews via Capacitor/Tauri

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be used in PWAs with manual setup

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; works with Electron/Tauri via WebViews

learning-curve.comment: Very gentle; minimal concepts, familiar HTML-based approach

concepts-to-learn.comment: x-data, directives (x-show, x-on, x-for), magic properties ($el, $refs), and plugins

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~1 minute; just add CDN script and write x-data in HTML

migration-path.comment: Upgrade guide from v2 to v3 available

incremental-adoption.comment: Designed for incremental adoption; sprinkle interactivity on any HTML page
